beast_rider.name: Beast Rider
description: ## Beast Rider
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/36›

The cavalier is defined not only by his dedication to his order or his skill on the battlefield, but also by the special relationship he maintains with his mount. Where some cavaliers are simply skilled with horses or well-trained knights, the beast rider spends his life in constant pursuit of the most perfect mount, forming bonds with greater, more powerful, and more exotic creatures.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A beast rider is proficient with light and medium armor, and with shields (with the exception of tower shields)."}
:::ab{l=1 head icon=power replace="Mount~expert trainer" action=Ability title="Exotic Mount (Ex)"}

A beast rider forms a bond with a strong, loyal companion that permits him to ride it as a mount. This mount functions as a druid's ‹sidekick/animal companion›, using the beast rider's level as his effective druid level. The animal chosen as a mount must be large enough to carry the beast rider (Medium or Large for a Small character; Large or Huge for a Medium character).

The beast rider does not take an armor check penalty on ‹skill/Ride› checks while riding his mount. The mount is always considered combat trained, and begins play with ‹feat/Endurance› as a bonus feat. A beast rider's mount does not gain the *share spells* special ability.
Each time the beast rider increases in level, he can choose to select a new, more impressive mount better suited to his increased power.
Anytime a feat or ability allows a mount to make a hoof attack, it can make a claw, slam, or other analogous attack instead.

::ab[Small Size]{next icon=power l1="Small-sized beast riders can choose a ‹companion/pony› or ‹companion/wolf› mount." l4="A Small beast rider can also choose an ‹companion/allosaurus›, ‹companion/ankylosaurus›, ‹companion/arsinoitherium›, ‹companion/aurochs›, ‹companion/bison›, ‹companion/boar›, ‹companion/brachiosaurus›, ‹companion/elephant›, ‹companion/glyptodon›, ‹companion/hippopotamus›, ‹companion/mastodon›, ‹companion/megaloceros›, ‹companion/riding »dog›, ‹companion/giant snapping turtle›, ‹companion/triceratops›, or ‹companion/tyrannosaurus›." l7="He can also choose a dinosaur (‹companion/deinonychus› or ‹companion/velociraptor›)."}

::ab[Medium Size]{next icon=power l1="Medium beast riders can choose a ‹companion/camel› or ‹companion/horse›." l4="A Medium beast rider can also choose an allosaurus, ankylosaurus, arsinoitherium, aurochs, bison, brachiosaurus, elephant, glyptodon, hippopotamus, ‹companion/lion›, mastodon, megaloceros, giant snapping turtle, ‹companion/tiger›, triceratops, or tyrannosaurus as his mount. Additional mounts might be available with GM approval." l7="A Medium beast rider can select any creature whose natural size is Large or Huge, provided that creature is normally available as a Medium-sized animal companion at 7th level (like a ‹companion/bear›). To generate statistics for such a mount, apply the following modifications: **Size** Large; **Ability Scores** Str +2, Dex -2, Con +2. Increase the damage of each of the mount's natural attacks ‹misc/by one die« size›." special="A beast rider cannot choose a mount that is not capable of bearing his weight, that has fewer than four legs, or that has a fly speed (although the GM may allow mounts with a swim speed in certain environments)."}

castellan.name: Castellan
description: ## Castellan
‹SOURCE Melee Tactics Toolbox/9›

A castellan is a cavalier who dedicates himself to the defense of a castle or walled city. Though not as celebrated as the cavaliers who ride forth to take on distant foes, the castellan is no less brave or important, for the castellan and his allies are the last line of defense for those who depend on the fortification for protection.

::ab[Castle Defender]{icon=def-boost l1="A castellan gains a +1 bonus to Armor Class and an additional +1 bonus on attack rolls when he is ‹rule/on higher ground›. He also gains an additional +1 bonus to Armor Class and on Reflex saves when he has ‹rule/cover› other than soft cover from other creatures." l6="A castellan gains ‹ability/evasion› (as a rogue) when he has cover (but not soft cover) against the source of the attack." l11="These bonuses increase by 1." l16="He gains ‹talent/improved evasion› instead of evasion." special="Both the evasion and improved evasion abilities work even when the castellan wears medium or heavy armor." replace=Mount}

::ab[Castle Lore]{icon=boost l3="The castellan gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (engineering), Knowledge (local), Knowledge (nobility), Perception, and Stealth checks when he is in urban terrain, including fortifications. A castellan in an urban environment can always act in a surprise round, though he remains ‹rule/flat-footed› until he acts." l8="The bonuses increase to +4." l13="The bonuses increase to +6." l18="The bonuses increase to +8." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Guard Companion]{l=4 head icon=power ability="The castellan forms a bond with an ‹sidekick/animal companion› that serves as a guard. While most castellans select birds or canines, the castellan can choose any animal companions available to ‹class/ranger«s›. This ability functions like the ‹class/druid› *nature bond* ability, except that the castellan's effective druid level is equal to his cavalier level - 3 and his animal companion doesn't gain the *share spells* special ability." replace="Expert trainer"}
;;;ab{title="Ranger Animal Companions" icon=power next action=Choice}
:::div{className="columnar clearBoth"}
- ‹companion/Antelope›
- ‹companion/Armorfish›
- ‹companion/Baboon›
- ‹companion/Badger›
- ‹companion/Bird« (Eagle, Hawk, or Owl)›
- ‹companion/Bustard›
- ‹companion/Camel›
- ‹companion/Capybara›
- ‹companion/Cheetah›
- ‹companion/Constrictor Snake›
- ‹companion/Dire Rat›
- ‹companion/Dog›
- ‹companion/Elk›
- ‹companion/Falcon›
- ‹companion/Giant Gecko›
- ‹companion/Hobbe Hound›
- ‹companion/Horse›
- ‹companion/Kangaroo›
- ‹companion/Leopard›
- ‹companion/Marsupial Devil›
- ‹companion/Pony›
- ‹companion/Ram›
- ‹companion/Reef Snake›
- ‹companion/Reindeer›
- ‹companion/Shark›
- ‹companion/Spitting Cobra›
- ‹companion/Stag›
- ‹companion/Stingray›
- [Swan, Trumpeter](companion/trumpeter_swan)

If the campaign takes place wholly or partly in an aquatic environment, you may choose from this list instead: ‹companion/Armorfish›, ‹companion/Reef Snake›, ‹companion/Shark›, ‹companion/Stingray›.

::ab[Mighty Defense]{l=11 icon=boost-melee ability="When the castellan ‹rule/readies an action› to make a melee attack against a creature that is not yet adjacent to him, he doubles the threat range of any melee weapons wielded during the attack. This increase doesn't stack with other effects that increase the threat range of his weapons. In addition, the cavalier can attempt a free ‹rule/dirty trick›, ‹rule/disarm›, ‹rule/sunder›, or ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver if his readied attack is successful. This free combat maneuver doesn't provoke an attack of opportunity." replace="Mighty charge"}
::ab[Defending Challenge]{icon=def l=12 passive="The castellan takes no penalty to Armor Class when using his challenge ability." replace="Demanding challenge"}

::ab[Supreme Defense]{l=20 icon=boost-melee ability="Whenever the castellan readies an action to make a melee attack against a creature that is not yet adjacent to him, his readied attack deals double the normal amount of damage (or triple the damage if using a ‹misc/brace› weapon against a charge). In addition, if the cavalier confirms a critical hit on the readied attack, the target is ‹misc/stunned› for 1d4 rounds. A successful Will save (DC = 10 + the cavalier's class level + the cavalier's Strength modifier) reduces this to being ‹misc/staggered› for 1d4 rounds." replace="Supreme charge"}

charger.name: Charger
description: ## Charger
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Monster Codex/5›

No warrior better epitomizes the centaur's devastating martial power than the charger. Born to the swiftest and most powerful of their kind, these natural cavaliers can achieve unparalleled feats of equestrian combat. Chargers rush headlong into the heart of any conflict to swiftly close on and smash into their enemies, channeling their power into their lances.
::prereq{other="‹monster/Centaur› (or any tauric creature at the GM's discretion)."}

::ab[Class Skills]{icon=skill note="A charger gains Acrobatics, Knowledge (nature), and Survival as class skills, but does not gain Climb, Handle Animal, or Ride as class skills." alter="Class skills"}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-boost note="A charger is proficient with all simple and martial weapons, with light and medium armor, and with shields (except tower shields)." special="A charger's cavalier levels stack with any fighter levels he possesses for the purpose of meeting the level prerequisite for feats that can specify a ‹eq-weapon/lance›, such as ‹feat/Weapon Specialization›." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Natural Mount (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A charger counts as having the ‹feat/Mounted Combat› feat only for the purpose of meeting feat prerequisites, and substitutes the Acrobatics skill for any Ride skill prerequisites when acquiring feats that have Mounted Combat as a prerequisite. Because of his equine form, a charger can wield a lance one-handed as if mounted and deals double damage with a lance while charging. A charger can never gain the benefit of the Mounted Combat feat (or other similar feats or effects, such as ‹feat/Trick Riding›) if ridden by another creature." replace=Mount}

::ab[Thundering Hooves (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost order=pa passive="A charger's hoof attack deals an additional 1d6 points of damage and its critical threat range increases to 19-20. This effect doesn't stack with any other effect that expands the critical threat range of a weapon." ability="Whenever he performs a ‹rule/charge›, the charger can make an additional hoof attack against his target with a -5 penalty on his attack roll." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Courser (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A charger's constant training increases his land speed by 10 feet. A charger can also move through up to 10 feet of ‹rule/difficult terrain› each round as if it were normal terrain." special="These benefits apply only when he is wearing no armor, light armor, or medium armor, and not carrying a heavy load. Apply this bonus before modifying the charger's speed because of any load carried or armor worn. These bonuses stack with any other bonuses to the charger's land speed or ability to ignore difficult terrain." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Ride Down (Ex)]{l=11 icon=melee free="A charger can attempt to ‹rule/overrun› any number of targets in the path of his charge. For each successful overrun combat maneuver check, the charger automatically deals an amount of damage to the overrun target equal to that of his hoof attack and can continue with his charge. If any overrun attempt fails during the charge, the charger's progress ends in the space directly in front of the target where the maneuver failed." special="These overrun attempts do not provoke attacks of opportunity. For each target in the path of his charge, the charger incurs a cumulative -2 penalty on his overrun combat maneuver checks." replace="Mighty charge"}

circuit_judge.name: Circuit Judge
description: ## Circuit Judge
‹SOURCE Path of the Hellknight/32›
A circuit judge claims a specific region, becoming an expert in the local laws and, perhaps, becoming a fearsome executioner.

::ab[Circuit (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost choice="A circuit judge chooses a single community. While in that community or within 25 miles of it, she adds half her cavalier level (minimum 1) on Intimidate, Knowledge (local), and Sense Motive skill checks (as well as any other law-related skill checks, at the GM's discretion)." order=cp passive="Whenever she advances in level, the range within which she gains this bonus expands by another 25 miles-50 miles at 2nd level, 75 miles at 3rd level, and so forth. This bonus on Intimidate checks increases by 2 when she enters a community within this area that she hasn't visited for 1 week or more." imp9="The circuit judge can select another community within the range in which she gains this ability's bonus. She can measure from that community (as well as previous communities) to determine whether she gains this ability's bonus on the relevant skill checks." imp17="The circuit judge can select a third community within the range, following the above restrictions and gaining the above's benefits." replace="Tactician~greater tactician~master tactician"}

:::ab{title="Sentence (Ex)" icon=power-boost-down usage="Twice per day at 1st level, plus two additional times per day for every 3 cavalier levels beyond 1st, to a maximum of 14 times per day at 19th level." action="Swift Action" replace=Challenge}

The circuit judge can choose one target within sight to *sentence.*@FN[1] The circuit judge gains the potential for a variety of bonuses related to that target. The circuit judge chooses the form of these benefits from the following list of inquisitor ‹ability/judgments›: destruction, justice, protection, purity, or smiting.

The benefits of these judgments apply only to the circuit judge and the target - the protection judgment's bonus to Armor Class, for example, applies only to attacks made by the *sentenced* target (not any creature who attacks the circuit judge while the judgment is active). Treat the circuit judge's level as her inquisitor level for the purposes of determining how these judgments increase in power. She can switch her *sentence* as a @HL[swift action], but doing so expends an additional daily use of this ability.
*Sentencing* a foe requires much of the circuit judge's concentration. The circuit judge takes a -2 penalty to her Armor Class, except against attacks made by the target of her *sentence.*

The *sentence* remains in effect either until the target is dead or unconscious or until the combat ends. *Sentences* are considered *challenges* for the purposes of the effects listed in the challenge section describing the cavalier's ‹ability/order<s›.

::ab[Double Jeopardy (Ex)]{l=12 icon=boost ability="Whenever a circuit judge *sentences* a foe, she can gain the benefits of two inquisitor judgments against the foe instead of one. Using this ability does not expend a daily use of *sentence.*" replace="Demanding challenge"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[The original text starts this ability with "Once per day as a swift action..." but later contradicts itself by saying "The circuit judge can use this ability twice per day at 1st level, plus..." This app takes the position that the second line overrides the first line. As always, consult your GM.]{from=1}

constable.name: Constable
description: ## Constable
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Streets/17›
Constables keep order in the narrow streets and dark alleys of settlements.
::ab[Class Skills]{icon=skill note="Perception is a class skill for constables." alter="The cavalier's class skills"}

::ab[Apprehend (Ex)]{icon=power-boost l1="A constable gains ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› as a bonus feat and can attempt a ‹rule/grapple› combat maneuver check in place of the attack at the end of a ‹rule/charge›." l2="The constable receives a +1 bonus on Perception checks and combat maneuver checks to ‹rule/disarm›, grapple, or ‹rule/trip› opponents." l4="The constable does not treat ‹rule/crowds› as difficult terrain." imp7="The bonus becomes +2." imp12="The bonus becomes +3." imp17="The bonus becomes +4." replace=Mount}

::ab[Squad Commander (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A constable can spend 1 minute laying out a plan to activate the *tactician* ability without having it count against his number of uses per day." special="The ability must be triggered within 1 hour of the plan being made, and the benefits last for 1 minute per cavalier level he has. The constable can have only one plan at a time, and if a new plan is made, any old plan is lost." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Quick Interrogator (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="A constable can attempt a Diplomacy check to gather information in 1d6 &times& 5 minutes and attempt a ‹skill/Diplomacy› or ‹skill/Intimidate› check to change someone's attitude in 5 rounds." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Badge (Ex)]{icon=def-boost l5="A constable's badge becomes a powerful symbol of rules and authority. As long as he wears his badge, allies within 30 feet who can see him gain a +2 morale bonus on saving throws against charm, compulsion, and fear effects and a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls against targets the constable has challenged." l10="These bonuses become +3 and +2, respectively." l15="These bonuses become +4 and +3." l20="These bonuses increase to +5 and +4." replace=Banner}

::ab[Instant Order (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power standard="A constable can bark an order to an ally within 30 feet. That ally can instantly take a single **move** or **standard** action to comply with the order. Taking the action ‹misc/daze«s› the ally for 1 round afterward." imp20="The constable can use this ability as a @HLmove_action, but no more than once per round." replace="Mighty charge~supreme charge"}

::ab[Greater Badge (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def standard="A constable can hold his badge aloft to inspire his allies to be steadfast. All allies within 30 feet gain a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to twice the constable's cavalier level for 10 minutes." special="A given ally can benefit from this ability only once per day." replace="Greater banner"}

courtly_knight.name: Courtly Knight
description: ## Courtly Knight
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/24›
Some cavaliers strive to be just as effective at the court of a local lord as on the field of battle. These courtly knights are equally at home in a duel with words or swords.

::ab[Social Presence (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A courtly knight gains a bonus on all Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, and Sense Motive checks. This bonus is equal to 1 + one-fourth of her cavalier level. A courtly knight starts any ‹rule/verbal duel<s› with an extra edge she can use for only the *presence* tactic unless she is at a significant or extreme disadvantage in the duel." replace=Tactician}

::ab[Imperious Attitude (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power flavor="A courtly knight develops a commanding demeanor, and the strength of her conviction is often enough to get others to do as she asks, merely because she expects it to be done." ability="In a social setting, the courtly knight can attempt Diplomacy checks to make a request of a creature whose attitude toward her is unfriendly, provided that creature does not intend to harm or oppose the courtly knight or her allies in the immediate future. Further, whenever she attempts a Diplomacy check to make a request, a courtly knight can first attempt an Intimidate check (DC = 20 + the creature's Charisma modifier). If she succeeds, she gains a +5 bonus on the subsequent Diplomacy check. Neither aspect of imperious attitude applies if the requested creature considers itself to be of an equal or higher social rank than the courtly knight, which includes creatures that don't understand or value the concept of social ranks." replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Grand Boast (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="Whenever a courtly knight uses her *challenge* class feature against an opponent who is at full health and under no negative afflictions, conditions, spells, or other effects, she can make a grand boast, declaring that she can defeat her foe with ease. She does not gain the benefits of her *challenge* (including any additional benefits granted by her *order*) when making a grand boast. If she successfully defeats the target of her *challenge* within 1 minute, however, she is vindicated in her boast and gains the benefits of her *challenge* against all allies of the defeated enemy who witnessed her grand boast and who are of the same or lower CR as the defeated enemy." special="If any of the courtly knight's allies attack the initial target of the *challenge* or assist the courtly knight after the boast, this ability has no effect." replace="Master tactician"}

daring_champion.name: Daring Champion
description: ## Daring Champion
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/90›

While many cavaliers are the champions of old fighting forms, some younger, more daring cavaliers mix a martial style influenced by the lighter armored and more flamboyant swashbuckler forms with the dedication of cavalier orders.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="Daring champions are proficient with all simple and martial weapons, light and medium armor, and bucklers." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Champion's Finesse (Ex)]{icon=power l=1 ability="A daring champion gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Weapon Finesse› feat with light or one-handed piercing melee weapons, and he can use Charisma in place of Intelligence for the purpose of combat feats prerequisites. A daring champion also counts as having the Weapon Finesse feat for the purpose of meeting feat requirements." replace=Mount}

::ab[Nimble (Ex)]{icon=def l3="A daring champion gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC when wearing light or no armor and carrying no more than a light load. Anything that causes the daring champion to lose his Dexterity bonus to AC also causes him to lose this dodge bonus." l7="The bonus becomes +2." l11="The bonus becomes +3." l15="The bonus is now +4." l19="The bonus increases to +5." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Panache and Deeds (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A daring champion gains the ‹class/swashbuckler«'s› panache class feature, along with the following swashbuckler deeds: ‹swashdeed/dodging panache›, ‹swashdeed/precise strike›, and ‹swashdeed/swashbuckler initiative›." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Advanced Deeds (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power ability="A daring champion gains the following swashbuckler deeds: ‹swashdeed/superior feint›, ‹swashdeed/targeted strike›, ‹swashdeed/subtle blade›, and ‹swashdeed/dizzying defense›." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Champion's Weapon Mastery (Ex)]{l=20 icon=melee-boost passive="When a daring champion threatens a critical hit with a light or one-handed piercing melee weapon, that critical is automatically confirmed. Furthermore, the critical modifier of those weapons increases by 1 (&times&2 becomes &times&3, for example)." replace="Supreme charge"}

daring_general.name: Daring General
description: ## Daring General
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/24›

Most cavaliers are great tacticians, but some stand above the rest for their ability to lead troops under their command, and inspire them to great accomplishments. Those cavaliers most gifted at command often become daring generals.

:::ab{l=6 title="Aides-de-Camp (Ex)" action=Ability head icon=power imp12="The daring general gains an additional cohort (though see Special below) and a number of additional followers equal to the number of followers he gained from the Leadership feat (doubling his followers)." imp18="The daring general gains a third cohort (though see Special below) and a number of additional followers equal to the number of followers he gained from the Leadership feat (ending up with triple the number of followers normally granted by Leadership)." special="All cohorts that the daring general gains from this ability must have levels only in classes that grant a full base attack bonus (such as barbarian, fighter, paladin, or ranger), and must be at least 4 levels lower than the daring general himself. When determining the kinds of cohorts the daring general can attract, he treats his Leadership score as 3 lower for each subsequent cohort (effectively taking a -3 penalty for the second cohort and a -6 penalty for the third cohort)." replace="Bonus feats gained at 6th, 12th, and 18th levels."}

A daring general gains a modified version of the ‹feat/Leadership› feat, even if he doesn't meet the prerequisites. All followers that the daring general gains as a result of this version of the Leadership feat can only have levels in the ‹npcclass/warrior› class.

Additionally, the daring general can grant special roles to his cohorts, as outlined below. Unless otherwise stated, only one cohort can fill any given role at any given time. Designating a cohort as filling a special role requires a 24-hour ceremonial ritual.

Beyond the rules noted above, if the daring general uses his followers to create an army using the ‹rule/mass combat› rules, that army starts with one extra ‹rule/army_>tactic<s› of the daring general's choice and a +2 bonus to its morale score.

The daring general can assign each of his followers individually to serve as either footmen or honor guards (described below). He does not need to assign either role to a given follower, if he prefers. He can change the role of a follower at any time, but it takes 1 day per level for the follower to change her tactics to suit her new role. Followers with a particular role must group together with other followers with the same role to form an army in mass combat.

::ab[Groom (Cohort Role)]{next icon=boost ability="The groom cares for the daring general's mount. She does not take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while riding the cavalier's mount. Additionally, once per day, the groom can spend 10 minutes preparing the daring general's mount for battle. If she does, the mount's speed increases by 10 feet and its Dexterity score increases by 2. These benefits last for 1 hour."}

::ab[Squire (Cohort Role)]{next icon=def ability="The squire's main task is to care for the daring general's arms and armor. If the squire aids the daring general in donning his armor, it takes half as much time to don as it would if someone else aided the daring general. Additionally, when the squire is adjacent to the daring general, as a @HLstandard_action, she can grant a +2 bonus to the daring general's AC against the first attack made against the daring general before the squire's next turn. This counts as an application of the ‹rule/aid another<_2› action, but the squire does not need to threaten the attacker in question."}

::ab[Standard Bearer (Cohort Role)]{next icon=power ability="The daring general's standard bearer can carry his banner in his stead, granting the benefits of his *banner* ability (and later, his *greater banner* ability) to all allies within 60 feet of the standard bearer. If the standard bearer becomes unconscious or dead, she ceases to grant these benefits."}

::ab[Footman (Follower Role)]{next icon=boost-down passive="Each follower gains a +2 bonus on attack and damage rolls, but takes a -2 penalty to her AC and on saving throws. A footman unit gains a +2 bonus to its OM and takes a -2 penalty to its DV in mass combat."}

::ab[Honor Guard (Follower Role)]{next icon=def-down passive="The follower gains a +2 bonus to her AC and on saving throws, but takes a -2 penalty on attack and damage rolls. An honor guard unit gains a +2 bonus to its DV and takes a -2 penalty to its OM in mass combat."}

::ab[Shared Challenge (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power-boost passive="When a daring general issues a *challenge,* his followers and cohorts take up that *challenge* alongside him. Whenever the daring general uses his *challenge* class feature, as long as the *challenge* remains in effect, each of the daring general's followers and cohorts that are within 30 feet of him gain a +4 morale bonus on attack rolls made against the target of his *challenge.*" replace="Demanding challenge"}

::ab[Supreme Tactician (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="At 20th level, whenever the daring general uses his *tactician* ability, he can grant any number of teamwork feats that he knows to his allies, and when he uses his *tactician* ability as a @HLstandard_action in combat, it does not count against his daily uses of *tactician.*" replace="Supreme charge"}

disciple_of_the_pike.name: Disciple of the Pike
description: ## Disciple of the Pike
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/13›
The ‹rule/Hellknight<s› ‹hkorder/Order of the Pike› are renowned monster hunters, taking on large game using ancient weapon arts combined with modern equipment.

::ab[Bigger They Are (Ex)]{icon=def passive="A disciple of the pike gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC against creatures larger than he is. This bonus increases by 1 if the creature is two size categories larger." imp6="The bonus increases to +3 against a creature three size categories larger." imp12="The bonus increases to +4 against creatures four size categories larger." replace=Mount}

::ab[Pike Charge (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def passive="A disciple of the pike learns to make more accurate ‹rule/charge› attacks with weapons from the polearms or spears ‹ability/fighter »weapon group<s›. He receives a +4 bonus on melee attacks when charging with a polearm or spear (instead of the normal +2). In addition, the disciple of the pike takes no penalty to his AC after making a charge attack with a polearm or spear." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Monster Hunter (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="A disciple of the pike has an uncanny ability to identify monsters. He can attempt Knowledge checks untrained to identify monsters and their weaknesses, and gains a bonus equal to half of his cavalier level on Knowledge checks to identify monsters." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-boost ability="A disciple of the pike gains weapon training, just like a ‹class/fighter›. He must select polearms or spears as his weapon group, and never gains another weapon group. His bonuses with the selected group otherwise progress as though his fighter level were equal to his cavalier level." replace="Banner~greater banner"}

::ab[Agile Charger (Ex)]{icon=boost l=11 passive="A disciple of the pike takes no penalties caused by ‹rule/difficult terrain› while charging. His movement is still hampered by obstacles, poor visibility, and other conditions. This ability doesn't allow him to move through impassible terrain." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Deadly Charge (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="Whenever a disciple of the pike makes a charge attack with a polearm or spear, he deals double the normal amount of damage. In addition, if he confirms a critical hit on a deadly charge, the target is ‹misc/stunned› for 1d4 rounds. If the target succeeds at a Will save (DC = 10 + the cavalier's base attack bonus), it is instead ‹misc/staggered› for 1d4 rounds." replace="Supreme charge"}

drakerider.name: Drakerider
description: ## Drakerider
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/24›

While many cavaliers dream of riding a dragon into battle, drakeriders come to learn that the reality involves far more effort than they had expected. Unlike other cavaliers, drakeriders must train their mounts from hatchlings, fighting and toiling alongside their drakes for years through countless struggles before the proud dragons are willing to accept them as riders. Some residents of Triaxus, envious of the close connection between dragonriders and dragons, attempt to emulate this relationship by raising drake hatchlings. These efforts aren't always successful, sometimes leaving the would-be drakeriders dead or maimed.

::ab[Drake Mount]{icon=power-down ability="A drakerider gains a ‹sidekick/drake« companion› instead of a mount. She gains cavalier's charge at 9th level instead of 3rd." replace="Mount~tactician~expert trainer~banner~greater tactician~greater banner~master tactician" alter="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Approved Order]{icon=info note="A drakerider can join only an order approved by its mount. Suitable orders include ‹order/order of the beast (ISC)›, ‹order/order of the cockatrice›, and ‹order/order of the dragon›, as well as other fitting orders at the GM's discretion."}

emissary.name: Emissary
description: ## Emissary
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/36›

Cavaliers serve many roles on the battlefield, from bold leaders and shock troops to dashing knights and mounted juggernauts. Some cavaliers, however, focus more on speed and mobility than they do on the raw power of the mounted charge. On foot or in the saddle, the emissary is usually first to meet the enemy, with a pointed lance, a drawn sword, or terms for surrender.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="An emissary does not gain proficiency with heavy armor."}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power passive="An emissary can select ‹feat/Endurance›, ‹feat/Fleet›, or ‹feat/Run› in addition to the list of combat feats whenever he gains a bonus feat."}

::ab[In or Out of the Saddle (Ex)]{icon=power-boost l=1 ability="An emissary gains ‹feat/Mounted Combat› as a bonus feat. In addition, he can move at normal speed when wearing medium armor." replace=Tactician}

::ab[Battlefield Agility (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="An emissary learns to be more aware of the threats that surround him in combat, and shares this awareness with his loyal steed. Both the emissary and his mount gain ‹feat/Mobility› as a bonus feat." replace=Banner}

::ab[Mounted Acrobatics (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power ability="An emissary is even more adept at hopping on and off his steed, and even dropping alongside its flanks mid-gallop. He gains ‹feat/Trick Riding› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. The emissary can employ this feat even when wearing medium armor." replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Mounted Dervish (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power-boost ability="An emissary becomes a fearsome, mobile foe on the battlefield. He gains ‹feat/Mounted Skirmisher› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. In addition, whenever the emissary is mounted and takes the ‹rule/charge› action, he adds 10 feet to his mount's speed." replace="Greater banner"}

::ab[Erratic Charge (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="An emissary learns to ride through the thick of combat, striking and then moving away with blinding speed. Whenever the emissary is mounted and takes the ‹rule/charge› action, he can first move 10 feet and make a melee attack. This first attack is not a charge, but a regular melee attack, and the movement counts toward the total allowed movement for the round. After making this attack, the emissary must still move in a straight line to reach the target of his charge, and he takes a -5 penalty on the attack roll for the attack at the end of his charge." replace="Supreme charge"}

esquire.name: Esquire
description: ## Esquire
‹SOURCE Cohorts and Companions/30›
The esquire is a roving knight who acquires the services of a faithful follower - an aide-de-camp-who serves as the esquire's assistant, spear carrier, and guard.
:::ab{l=3 action=Ability title="Aide-de-Camp (Ex)" icon=power replace=Mount}}

An esquire acquires the services of a loyal aide-de-camp. This NPC functions similarly to a cohort (as the ‹feat/Leadership› feat), though the aide-de-camp must advance in a class that grants proficiency with all martial weapons and cannot multiclass until the esquire reaches 7th level (though the aide-de-camp can select an archetype). If the aide-de-camp is a cavalier, he must follow the same order as the esquire.

The aide-de-camp gains ‹feat/Swift Aid› as a bonus feat, and provides a +2 (rather than +1) bonus when using this feat to aid the esquire. As a @HL[move action,] the aide-de-camp can retrieve a stored item or an item on the ground and hand it to the esquire. The esquire does not have to take an action to receive the item, though he must be conscious and have a free hand. This action does not provoke attacks of opportunity for the aide-de-camp or esquire.

If the aide-de-camp dies performing an act that directly supports the edicts of the esquire's order (GM's discretion), the esquire does not take a penalty to his Leadership score. If an aide-de-camp dies, or is released from the esquire's service, the esquire may gain a new aide-de-camp by scouting for potential candidates in a city or large town. This requires 1 day per 5 levels of the aide-de-camp to be recruited.

::ab[Teamwork (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power passive="As long as an esquire and his aide-de-camp are adjacent, the aide-de-camp is treated as having any ‹main/teamwork feat«s› the esquire has. They also share any teamwork feats that function when two or more allies threaten the same target, as long as they both threaten the same target." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Shared Challenge (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="Whenever an esquire challenges an opponent when his aide-de-camp is within 30 feet, he may grant his aide-de-camp bonuses equal to one-half of the bonuses he gains from *challenge,* including bonuses derived from the cavalier's order. If the aide-de-camp is also a cavalier and challenges the same target, these bonuses do not stack. Like the esquire, his aide-decamp takes a -2 penalty to Armor Class against attacks from creatures other than the challenged opponent." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Banner (Ex)]{icon=power-boost passive="An aide-de-camp can carry an esquire's banner for him and still grant allies within range the banner's bonuses. While doing so the aide-de-camp gains double the normal morale bonuses provided by the banner." alter=Banner}

::ab[Avenge Me (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power passive="Whenever the esquire is killed, dying, stunned, dazed, or otherwise unable to take actions, his aide-de-camp gains the full benefits of the esquire's *challenge* and *order* abilities against all creatures that attacked the esquire in the previous round. If the aide-de-camp is also a cavalier and challenges the same target, these bonuses do not stack. These benefits last until the esquire regains the ability to take actions, or until the aide-de-camp or the triggering opponents are killed." special="If his aide-de-camp is killed, dying, or otherwise unable to take actions the esquire may declare one creature of his choice that attacked the aide-de-camp in the past round as his *quarry;* this functions identically to the ‹class/ranger› class ability. These bonuses last until the aide-de-camp recovers or until the cavalier or the triggering opponents are killed." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Fight As One (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="The *shared challenge* ability increases in power, granting the aide-de-camp full benefit of the esquire's *challenge.* In addition, if the esquire and aide-de-camp both confirm a critical hit against the same target within the same round, the target must make a Fortitude save (DC 10 +1/2 the esquire's cavalier level + the esquire's Charisma bonus) or be ‹misc/staggered› for 1d4 rounds." replace="Supreme charge"}

fell_rider.name: Fell Rider
description: ## Fell Rider
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/122›
The fell rider rides a bestial steed, a mount mastered by him alone. He tramples his enemies down, leaving twisted bodies in his wake, and fear rides with him.
::prereq{r=Hobgoblin}
::ab[Brute Steed (Ex)]{icon=boost-down passive="A fell rider's mount is unusually large and fierce. It gains a +2 bonus to Strength, but takes a -2 penalty to Dexterity." alter=Mount}

::ab[Rampage (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost ability="A fell rider gains ‹feat/Trample› as a bonus feat. The fell rider receives a +2 bonus on ‹rule/overrun› attempts made while mounted. In addition, his mount gains a +2 bonus on damage rolls when making hoof attacks or using the ‹umr/trample› monster ability, even when not overrunning." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Dread Rider (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-power order=pf passive="A fell rider gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his level on Intimidate checks made while mounted." free="Once per round, he can make an Intimidate check to demoralize a foe he has trampled or charged while mounted." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Fell Presence (Ex)]{icon=lower-aura l5="A fell rider inspires dread while mounted. As long as the fell rider is mounted, all enemies within 60 feet receive a -2 penalty on savings throws against fear." l10="The penalty becomes -3." l15="The penalty becomes -4." l20="The penalty becomes -5." replace=Banner}

::ab[Deadly Rampage (Ex)]{icon=def-melee l=11 ability="A mounted fell rider no longer provokes attacks of opportunity when he attempts to overrun a creature. He can make a single melee attack against the creature as a @HLfree_action if the overrun attempt succeeds." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Terror (Ex)]{l=14 icon=lower-aura standard="A fell rider can rear up his mount, spreading terror among his enemies. Enemies within 60 feet who can see the fell rider must succeed at a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 the fell rider's level + the fell rider's Charisma modifier) or be ‹misc/shaken› for 1 round per level the fell rider possesses. Creatures with half or fewer Hit Dice than the fell rider become ‹misc/frightened› instead. An opponent that succeeds at the saving throw is immune to that fell rider's terror ability for 24 hours." special="This ability is a mind-affecting fear effect." replace="Greater banner"}

::ab[Unstoppable Rampage (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power-melee-boost ability="A mounted fell rider can overrun up to four creatures as part of a single overrun combat maneuver. Each attempt made against a creature after the first receives a cumulative -5 penalty (for example, the fourth overrun attempt would be at a -15 penalty). If any overrun attempt fails, the fell rider cannot make further overrun attempts that round. Attacks made by the fell rider or his mount as part of the overrun, such as those provided by the Trample feat or the *deadly rampage* ability, receive a +4 bonus to hit, and any critical threats from these attacks automatically confirm." replace="Supreme charge"}

first_mothers_fang.name: First Mother's Fang
description: ## First Mother's Fang
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/17›

Nagajor legend states that when ‹faith/Nalinivati› created the first ‹race/nagaji›, she chose a paragon of their kind to instruct in the use of exotic weapons, lore, and words so the creature could best serve her as a bodyguard and emissary: the original first mother's fang. Whether or not these paragons descend from a single nagaji, first mother's fangs of Nagajor have expanded their numbers over the generations, and they no longer speak with the full authority of the First Mother. In modern times, a first mother's fang acts as a servant of his nation, a skilled general and tactician in times of war and a noble governor during times of peace.
::ab[Class Skills]{icon=skill note="A first mother's fang adds all Knowledge skills to his list of class skills." alter="The cavalier's class skills"}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power note="A first mother's fang is proficient with all simple and martial weapons. Additionally, a first mother's fang is proficient in one exotic melee weapon of his choice and one exotic ranged weapon of his choice." alter="The cavalier's weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Serpent Mount (Ex)]{icon=power l1="Instead of the usual choices for a cavalier's mount, first mother's fangs ride on giant riding constrictors. These use the statistics of the ‹companion/constrictor snake› druid animal companion, except that they are suitable mounts and begin as size Large at 1st level with no other adjustments to their abilities or statistics based on this size change. A serpent mount is considered to be a quadruped for the purpose of determining its ‹rule/carrying capacity›, and its base land speed is 40 feet (other speeds are unaffected)." l4="A giant riding constrictor doesn't increase in size again, but it does receive all the other usual 4th-level benefits." alter=Mount}

::ab[Honored Warrior (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-boost ability="A first mother's fang is expected to act as a military leader in wartime and a governor in peace time, and as a result, he must be versed both in the martial and political arts. He gains ‹feat/Combat Expertise› as a bonus feat, and if his Intelligence score is less than 13, it counts as 13 for the sole purpose of meeting the prerequisites of combat feats that require Combat Expertise as a prerequisite." imp2="The first mother's fang gains a ‹class/vigilante› social talent and a bonus ‹main/combat feat›. When selecting his vigilante social talent, a first mother's fang can select ‹soctalent/celebrity discount›, ‹soctalent/celebrity perks›, ‹soctalent/great renown›, ‹soctalent/incredible renown›, ‹soctalent/instant recognition›, ‹soctalent/loyal aid›, ‹soctalent/renown›, ‹soctalent/social grace›, or ‹soctalent/triumphant return›. He treats his cavalier level as his effective vigilante level when determining whether he meets a talent's prerequisites, as well as its effects. He gains the improved attitude and Intimidate bonuses from *renown* as if he were in both social and vigilante identities. When selecting skills with *social grace,* he must choose from the following list: Appraise, Bluff, Diplomacy, Intimidate, Knowledge (geography, history, local, or nobility), Perform, Profession (soldier), or Sense Motive. He is always considered to be in his social identity for the purpose of *social grace* (though he continues to receive the benefits for both identities from *renown*). When selecting his bonus combat feat, he can choose from only combat feats that list Combat Expertise as a prerequisite." imp8="He gains a new social talent and combat feat, following the same restrictions." imp15="He gains a new social talent and combat feat, following the same restrictions." replace=Order}

gallant.name: Gallant
description: ## Gallant
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/12›
Gallants embody the virtues of honor, generosity, and civility. Their personal symbol serves as an inspiration to others in a courtly setting as much as on a battlefield.

::ab[Code of Gallantry]{icon=info-warning note="A gallant must belong to either the ‹order/order of the blue rose›, the ‹order/order of the guard›, the ‹order/order of the lion›, or the ‹order/order of the sword›. A gallant must also be of lawful good or neutral good alignment. A gallant who ceases to be lawful good or neutral good, or who violates his order's edicts, loses all class features except armor and weapon proficiencies until he restores his alignment or atones for his violation (see the ‹spell/atonement› spell), as appropriate." alter="The cavalier's order"}

::ab[Symbol of Inspiration (Ex)]{l=5 flavor="The gallant's personal crest becomes an inspirational symbol of his chivalrous ideals." icon=def-aura passive="As long as he displays his crest on his tabard, banner, or similar item, allies within 60 feet who can see him gain a morale bonus on saving throws against charm, compulsion, and fear effects, and a bonus on damage rolls to deal nonlethal damage. Both of these bonuses are equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of his cavalier level." replace=Banner}

::ab[Symbol of Resilience (Su)]{l=14 icon=def flavor="The gallant's personal crest empowers his allies to stand firm against evil." standard="While his personal crest is displayed, the gallant can bolster his allies' resilience. All allies within 60 feet of the gallant gain DR 5/evil for 1 minute. An ally cannot benefit from this ability more than once per day." replace="Greater banner"}

gendarme.name: Gendarme
description: ## Gendarme
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/37›

The gendarme cares less for the finer points of tactical precision than he does for the exhilaration of the charge: the rush of wind through the visor of his helmet, the feel of his couched lance, the satisfying shriek of armor giving way before his weapon's force as the point drives past metal into his foes.

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power ability="A gendarme trains to be a mounted terror, almost to the exclusion of all other abilities. He gains bonus feats at 1st level, 5th level, and then every three levels thereafter, but must select these bonus feats from the following list: ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush›, ‹feat/Mounted Combat›, ‹feat/Power Attack›, ‹feat/Ride-By Attack›, ‹feat/Spirited Charge›, ‹feat/Spring Attack›, and ‹feat/Unseat›." special="If the gendarme has already selected all of the listed feats, then he may select his bonus feats from those feats listed as ‹main/combat feat«s›." replace="Tactician~greater tactician~master tactician~cavalier bonus feats"}

::ab[Transfixing Charge (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="A gendarme represents the epitome of mounted combat. Whenever he makes a ‹rule/charge› attack while mounted, he deals triple the normal damage (quadruple if using a ‹eq-weapon/lance›); this damage includes all increases from the Spirited Charge feat and from the use of a lance. In addition, if the gendarme confirms a critical hit on a charge attack while mounted, the attack deals maximum damage for the weapon wielded. Additional damage from weapon properties, magic effects, precision-based bonuses, or other increases are rolled normally." replace="Supreme charge"}

ghost_rider.name: Ghost Rider
description: ## Ghost Rider
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/117›

Ghost riders are conflicted warriors who use the powers of the phantasmal dead even while seeking out and banishing the corruption of undeath from plagued communities. Their strange abilities mark them as outsiders and stoic loners, and they stay in one place only long enough to help the afflicted before riding off to unknown destinations.
:::ab{l=1 icon=power action=Ability title="Ghost Mount (Su)" replace=Mount}

A ghost rider gains the ability to manifest a special mount of conjured ectoplasm rather than one of flesh and bone. This spectral companion functions like a spiritualist's ‹sidekick/phantom› companion, using the ghost rider's cavalier level as her effective ‹class/spiritualist› level. The phantom takes the form of a ghostly creature analogous to one of a cavalier's normal mount options for her size and class level, such as a Large horse for a Medium ghost rider or a wolf for a Small ghost rider. A ghost rider must choose a mount that is capable of bearing her weight, that has four or more legs, and that lacks a fly speed (although the GM may allow mounts with a swim speed in certain environments). The GM might approve other animals as suitable mounts.

The ghost rider can manifest her ghost mount only in ectoplasmic form, and she gains the spiritualist's *etheric tether* class feature, applied to her ghost mount. The mount shares the cavalier's alignment. Though the mount cannot speak, it understands its master's verbal commands and gains the phantom's *link* ability. The ghost mount has ‹misc/good« Fortitude and Reflex» saves›, and can have ranks in any of the following skills: Acrobatics (Dex), Climb (Str), Escape Artist (Dex), Fly (Dex), Intimidate (Cha), Perception (Wis), Stealth (Dex), Survival (Wis), and Swim (Str). The ghost rider's ghost mount does not gain the *share spells, deliver touch spells,* or *incorporeal flight* abilities. Ghost mounts do not gain emotional focus abilities.
The ghost rider does not take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while riding her mount. The mount is always considered combat trained, and begins play with ‹feat/Endurance› as a bonus feat.

@HL[Ghost Mount Starting Statistics:] **Type** animal (phantom); **Size** one size category larger than the ghost rider; **Speed** 50 ft.; **AC** +4 natural armor; **Attack** bite (1d4), 2 hooves (1d6; this is a secondary natural attack); **Ability Scores** Str 16, Dex 13, Con 15, Int 2, Wis 12, Cha 6.

::ab[Frightful Gaze (Su)]{l=1 icon=zap useMod=Cha standard="A ghost rider can use this ability on opponents within 30 feet, which acts as a ‹umr/gaze› attack until the ghost rider's next turn. Creatures within range that meet the ghost rider's gaze must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the ghost rider's class level + the ghost rider's Charisma modifier) or stand ‹rule/paralyzed› in fear for 1 round. This is a mind-affecting fear effect. Creatures that successfully save against that ghost rider's frightful gaze are immune to it for 24 hours." imp9="This ability can affect creatures that are mindless or immune to mind-affecting effects, though it still counts as a fear effect." replace="Tactician~greater tactician~master tactician"}

::ab[Fearless (Su)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A ghost rider becomes immune to fear. Each ally within 10 feet of the ghost rider gains a +4 morale bonus on saving throws against fear effects. This ability functions only while the ghost rider is conscious, not if she is unconscious or dead." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Spirited Mount (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost-magic l5="The ghost rider's mount ignores ‹rule/difficult terrain›." l8="It gains the ability to use ‹spell/water walk› at will." replace="Expert trainer~banner"}

::ab[Ghost Wind (Su)]{l=11 icon=magic-power l11="The ghost rider's ghost mount can use ‹spell/air walk› (as the spell, no action required) at will for up to 1 round at a time, after which it falls to the ground." l14="The mount can fly at its speed with a bonus on Fly skill checks equal to the ghost rider's class level." replace="Mighty charge~greater banner"}

green_knight.name: Green Knight
description: ## Green Knight
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/44›
Some knights serve nature itself and can be found protecting unspoiled natural places or serving fey beings.

::ab[Beast Tongue (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A green knight can use Diplomacy to improve an animal's attitude. *Beast tongue* otherwise functions like the druid's ‹ability/wild empathy› ability, using the green knight's Diplomacy modifier and using her cavalier level as her effective druid level, and it counts as that ability for the purpose of other effects (such as feat prerequisites or effects that alter or improve *wild empathy*)." replace=Tactician}
::ab[Implacable Knight (Ex)]{icon=power l=1 ability="A green knight gains ‹feat/Endurance› and ‹feat/Diehard› as bonus feats." replace=Mount}
::ab[Nature's Servant (Ex)]{icon=info note="A green knight must select the ‹order/order of the Green›." alter=Order}

::ab[Ferocious (Ex)]{icon=boost-def l3="A green knight is not staggered when below 0 hit points." l9="Taking actions when below 0 hit points does not cause her to lose hit points." replace="Cavalier's charge"}
::ab[Woodland Stride (Ex)]{icon=power l4="A green knight gains the druid's ‹ability/woodland stride› ability." replace="Expert trainer~banner"}

::ab[Stalwart (Ex)]{icon=def l9="Whenever a green knight succeeds at a Fortitude or Will save against an effect that has a reduced effect on a successful save, she instead negates the effect." replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Oaken Vitality (Su)]{icon=def flavor="Nature protects a green knight from many afflictions." l11="The green knight becomes immune to disease, ‹rule/infestations›, and poison." replace="Mighty charge~greater banner"}

::ab[Take Their Heads (Su)]{icon=boost flavor="A green knight's blade is blessed by mighty fey powers and unerringly seeks out foes' necks." l17="Any slashing weapon a green knight wields gains the ‹magic-enh/vorpal› weapon special ability." replace="Master tactician"}

::ab[Indestructible (Su)]{icon=boost-def l20="A green knight is virtually impervious to death. She gains a +6 bonus to her Constitution score. In addition, the green knight becomes immune to death effects and to effects that would kill her without reducing her to 0 hit points, unless the effect involves decapitation. If she is decapitated, the green knight can continue to act normally. Reattachment requires the head and body to be joined and the application of any effect that restores hit points." replace="Supreme charge"}

herald_squire.name: Herald Squire
description: ## Herald Squire
‹SOURCE Knights of the Inner Sea/21›
The herald squire is the eyes and ears of a traveling knight, often arriving ahead of her liege to bring word of his arrival, find lodgings, and get the lay of the land.

::ab[Introduction (Ex)]{l=1 icon=roll-power passive="Whenever a herald squire tries to influence a creature's attitude with the Diplomacy skill for the first time, she may roll her Diplomacy check twice and take the best result. In addition, any improvement of a creature's attitude toward the herald squire also applies to her knight." replace=Tactician}

::ab[Fleet of Foot (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-boost ability="A herald squire gains *fast movement,* as the ‹class/barbarian› class feature of the same name. If the herald squire gains *fast movement* from another class, the bonuses to her speed do not stack. Whenever she makes long ‹rule/overland «journeys<movement› alone or with only her mount, the distance a herald squire can travel each day increases by 20%." replace="The 2nd-level order ability"}

::ab[Transcend Language (Sp)]{l=3 usage="Three times/day." icon=magic ability="A herald squire can cast ‹spell/tongues› on herself, using her herald squire level as her caster level. The effects of this ability last for a number of rounds equal to her herald squire level." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

honor_guard.name: Honor Guard
description: ## Honor Guard
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/37›

Certain cavaliers are trained not as advance combatants, but as loyal guards, standing as firm defenders in the face of threats to their chosen charge. These honor guards are sometimes merely ornamental, performing their functions on a ceremonial basis, but a surprising number are capable, deadly opponents, able to take down a threat long before their liege knows of the danger. To be an honor guard is to constantly place one's self in harm's way, and to always be willing to lay down one's life at a moment's notice.

::ab[Sworn Defense (Ex)]{l=1 icon=down-def passive="Whenever an honor guard issues a challenge, he can select one ally as his ward for the duration of the challenge. Whenever the honor guard is adjacent to his ward, he takes a -1 penalty to Armor Class, and the ward receives a +1 dodge bonus to AC." alter=Challenge}

::ab[Intercept (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-def ability="An honor guard learns to better disrupt the attacks of his enemies. He gains ‹feat/Bodyguard› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisite. In addition, whenever the honor guard uses the ‹rule/aid another<_2› action to increase an ally's Armor Class, the bonus to Armor Class granted by the aid another action increases by +1." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Warding Charge (Ex)]{l=11 icon=def flavor="An honor guard can flash across the battlefield to protect his ward against the target of his challenge." immediate="Whenever the target of the honor guard's challenge makes an attack against his ward, the honor guard can move up to his speed and make a single melee attack against the target of his challenge. This movement and attack can be made as a ‹rule/charge› if the movement qualifies." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Defensive Challenge (Ex)]{l=12 icon=lower passive="Whenever an honor guard declares a challenge, his target must pay particular attention to him and his ability to intercept attacks. As long as the target is within the threatened area of the cavalier, it takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls against anyone other than the honor guard." replace="Demanding challenge"}

hooded_knight.name: Hooded Knight
description: ## Hooded Knight
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/26›

Some cavaliers wander the lonely roads of the world - any world, including the First World - protecting travelers and acting as their honor guard. Like the Eldest they usually venerate, they wear heavy hoods and rarely show their faces. Hooded knights are usually members of the ‹order/order of the blossom›, the ‹order/order of the shield›, or the ‹order/order of the sword›.
::ab[Class Skills]{icon=skill note="A hooded knight gains Knowledge (geography) and Survival as class skills instead of Climb and Swim." alter="The cavalier's class skills"}

::ab[Feytouched Mount]{icon=power note="A hooded knight's mount has the ‹template/fey-touched› simple template when he is riding it, but it cannot use its *change shape* ability while being ridden." alter="The cavalier's mount"}

::ab[Hero of the Roads (Ex)]{icon=boost l=1 passive="When on a road, a hooded knight gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks. A hooded knight and his mount can ‹rule/hustle› while traveling on a road without taking damage for 2 hours each day." imp5="He can now hustle for 4 hours without taking damage." imp9="His bonuses increase from +2 to +4. In addition, the speed of any mount a hooded knight rides is increased by 10 feet. By spending 1 minute coaxing his allies' mounts and expending one use of his challenge ability, a hooded knight can grant the same speed increase to the mounts ridden by all allies within 30 feet who can see and hear him. This speed increase to his allies' mounts lasts for 1 hour." imp10="He can now hustle for 6 hours without taking damage." imp15="He can now hustle for 8 hours without taking damage." imp17="His bonuses increase from +4 to +6. In addition, the hooded knight's mount's speed is now increased by 20 feet, and he can give this increased speed to his allies' mounts, as above." imp20="He can now hustle for 10 hours without taking damage." replace=Tactician}

::ab[Champion of the Roads (Sp)]{l=9 icon=magic ability="A hooded knight gains ‹spell/dimension door› as a spell-like ability with a caster level equal to his cavalier level. If the hooded knight is riding a mount, his mount does not count against the number of creatures he brings along with this ability. If the destination is not a road, the hooded knight is shunted to the nearest open space on a road, taking damage as described in the *dimension door* spell." useInc="4~cavalier~9" replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Paragon of the Roads (Sp)]{l=17 icon=boost ability="When hooded knight uses his *champion of the roads* ability, he can treat the ability as ‹spell/greater teleport› rather than *dimension door.* The hooded knight's mount and the mounts ridden by any creatures he brings along do not count against the number of teleported creatures." replace="Master tactician"}

huntmaster.name: Huntmaster
description: ## Huntmaster
‹SOURCE Animal Archive/6›
Huntmasters train the beasts favored by lordly castes into swift and deadly trackers.
::ab[Class Skills]{icon=skill note="A huntmaster gains Knowledge (nature), Stealth, and Survival as class skills."}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power note="A huntmaster is not proficient with heavy armor. Huntmasters are proficient with all simple and martial weapons, plus the ‹eq-weapon/net› and ‹eq-weapon/whip›."}

:::ab{l=1 icon=power-boost title="Hunting Pack (Ex)" action=Ability order=Cp passive="A huntmaster's animal companions gain a bonus on Survival checks to follow tracks equal to 1/2 the cavalier's class level (minimum 1)." replace=Mount}

A huntmaster forms a close bond with an animal companion. This animal companion functions as a druid's ‹ability/animal companion›, using the cavalier's level as his effective druid level. He can choose either a ‹companion/bird› or a ‹companion/dog› when selecting his animal companion.

The huntmaster can have more than one ‹sidekick/animal companion›, but he must divide up his effective druid level between his companions to determine the abilities of each companion. Each time a huntmaster's effective druid level increases, he must decide how to allocate the increase among his animal companions (including the option of adding a new 1st-level companion). Once an effective druid level is allocated to a particular companion, it cannot be redistributed while that companion is in the huntmaster's service (he must release a companion or wait until a companion dies to allocate its levels to another companion). The *share spells* animal companion ability does not give the huntmaster the ability to cast a single spell so that it affects all of his animal companions.

::ab[Bestial Challenge (Ex)]{icon=boost-down passive="Whenever a huntmaster issues a challenge, both he and his *hunting pack* gain the bonuses and penalties associated with his challenge." alter=Challenge}
::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{icon=info note="A huntmaster's tactician ability affects only his *hunting pack,* not other allies." alter="Tactician~greater tactician~master tactician"}

::ab[Takedown (Ex)]{l=3 icon=melee ability="A huntmaster's dog can make a free ‹rule/trip› or ‹rule/dirty trick› (‹rule/entangled›) combat maneuver after a successful melee attack. A huntmaster's bird can make a free dirty trick (‹misc/dazzled› or ‹misc/deafened›) combat maneuver after a successful melee attack; if the target is already dazzled, the bird can choose dirty trick (‹misc/blinded›) instead. These combat maneuvers do not provoke attacks of opportunity." replace="Cavalier's charge"}
::ab[Animal Trainer (Ex)]{l=4 icon=info ability="A huntmaster's *expert trainer* ability applies to birds and dogs rather than mounts." alter="Expert trainer"}
::ab[Swift Tracker (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A huntmaster gains the swift tracker ability, as the ‹class/ranger› ability of the same name." replace=Banner}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l6="A huntmaster and all members of his *hunting pack* gain ‹feat/Step Up› as a bonus feat." l12="They gain ‹feat/Following Step›." l18="They gain ‹feat/Step Up and Strike›." replace="Cavalier bonus feats"}

::ab[Pack Attack (Ex)]{l=11 icon=melee-boost ability="Whenever a huntmaster and any of his *hunting pack* or multiple animals in his *hunting pack* are adjacent and attack the same target in melee, they are treated as ‹rule/flanking›. In addition, they can flank a creature with ‹ability/improved uncanny dodge› if the huntmaster has at least 4 more levels than the target." replace="Mighty charge"}
::ab[Quarry (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power ability="A huntmaster gains *quarry,* as the ranger ability of the same name. The benefits of this ability also apply to his hunting pack." replace="Greater banner"}

::ab[Improved Quarry (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="A huntmaster gains *improved quarry,* as the ranger ability of the same name. The benefits of this ability also apply to his hunting pack." replace="Supreme charge"}

hussar.name: Hussar
description: ## Hussar
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/25›

Agile and maneuverable cavalry, hussars make up for their relatively light armor with their speed and cunning. They are known for doggedly pursuing their foes even in tight spaces, across rooftops, and in other places that mounted cavalry could normally never reach.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A hussar is proficient with light armor, but not with medium or heavy armor or with shields." replace="The cavalier's normal armor proficiencies"}
::ab[Agile Warrior (Ex)]{icon=skill note="A hussar gains Acrobatics, Escape Artist, Stealth, and Survival as class skills." alter="The cavalier's class skills"}

::ab[Fast Mount (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="A hussar is able to spur on any mount that he rides to faster speeds than other riders." passive="The base speed of any creature that the hussar is riding increases by 10 feet, plus an additional 5 feet for every five cavalier levels he possesses. This applies to all forms of movement that the mount possesses." replace="Tactician~banner~greater banner"}

::ab[Skillful Rider (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost flavor="A hussar and his mount work in tandem to achieve amazing accomplishments of dexterity while riding." passive="As long as the hussar is mounted, whenever he or his mount is required to attempt an Acrobatics, Climb, Escape Artist, Fly, Stealth, or Swim check, he can use his own modifier or his mount's, whichever is higher. Additionally, regardless of whose bonus is used, the hussar gains an additional +2 bonus on all skill checks to overcome obstacles as part of a chase." imp12="The bonus increases to +4." imp18="The bonus increases to +6." replace="Bonus feats gained at 6th, 12th, and 18th levels."}

::ab[Maneuverable Mount (Ex)]{l=9 flavor="A hussar is able to maneuver his mount with exceptional grace and poise, even in areas that would normally be a difficult fit for such a creature." icon=boost-def passive="Any creature that the hussar is riding can ‹misc/squeeze› into a space that is at least half as wide as its normal space without taking the normal penalties on attack rolls and AC for doing so, though it still moves at half speed." special="If the mount attempts to squeeze into a space less than half its space's width, it must use the Escape Artist skill to do so, and takes the normal penalties." replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Sudden Swerve (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power flavor="A hussar's control over his mount is advanced enough to allow the mount to turn on the spot, even when charging forward at full speed." ability="Whenever the hussar makes the ‹rule/charge› or ‹rule/run› action while mounted, he can make a single turn of up to 90 degrees at any one point during this movement." replace="Master tactician"}

knight_of_arnisant.name: Knight of Arnisant
description: ## Knight of Arnisant
‹SOURCE Armor Master's Handbook/5›

The cavaliers known as knights of Arnisant revere the famous general who was crucial in the defeat of ‹monster/the Whispering Tyrant›, the powerful wizard-king. Knights of Arnisant call upon Arnisant to grant them protection from evil magic, just as the Shield of Aroden protected him. Many of these cavaliers further emulate him by joining the ‹order/order of the shield›. Most knights of Arnisant serve at Lastwall, where the movement originated, but they can be found everywhere from the general's homeland of Taldor to the front lines of the Worldwound.

::ab[Shield Expertise]{l=1 icon=power ability="A knight of Arnisant gains the ‹feat/Shield Focus› feat as a bonus feat. His cavalier levels count as fighter levels for the purpose of qualifying for all feats that list the Shield Focus feat as a prerequisite." replace=Tactician}

::ab[Deflective Shield (Ex)]{l=4 flavor="A knight of Arnisant specializes in using his shield to deflect attacks." icon=def passive="He adds a bonus equal to his shield bonus to AC to his touch AC and his CMD, up to a maximum of half his cavalier level. He does not include any enhancement bonus he has from a magic shield in this calculation." replace="Expert trainer"}
::ab[Heraldic Banner (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A knight of Arnisant uses his shield as a *banner,* though its benefits apply only to allies within 30 feet." alter=Banner}

::ab[Soul Shield (Su)]{l=9 icon=def passive="When carrying a shield, a knight of Arnisant adds a bonus equal to his shield bonus to AC on all saving throws against curse spells and effects, up to a maximum of half his cavalier level. He does not include any enhancement bonus from a magic shield in this calculation." replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Defensive Challenge (Ex)]{l=12 icon=lower passive="Whenever a knight of Arnisant declares a *challenge,* his target must pay particular attention to him. As long as the target is within the threatened area of the cavalier, it takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls against anyone other than the knight of Arnisant." replace="Demanding challenge"}

::ab[Heart Shield (Su)]{l=17 icon=def passive="When carrying a shield, a knight of Arnisant adds a bonus equal to his shield bonus to AC on saving throws against death spells and magical death effects, up to a maximum of half his cavalier level. He does not include any enhancement bonus from a magic shield in this calculation." replace="Greater tactician"}

luring_cavalier.name: Luring Cavalier
description: ## Luring Cavalier
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/38›

Those who study the perfection of strategy and tactics know that picking the battlefield can grant advantages that only overwhelming numbers of allies can eclipse. The luring cavalier is a special type of cavalier that helps accomplish that goal. Typically, he uses a bow or other ranged weapon to lure opponents toward him, and then makes strategic retreats to position his enemies exactly where he wants them.

::ab[Far Challenge (Ex)]{icon=ranged-boost-down useInc="3~cavalier~1" swift="A luring cavalier can use his *far challenge* ability. When he does, the luring cavalier chooses one target within sight to *challenge.* The luring cavalier's ranged attacks deal extra damage whenever the attacks are made against the target of his challenge. This extra damage is equal to the luring cavalier's level." order=uwap ability="Additionally, once per day, the luring cavalier may spend a use of his *far challenge* ability to double the potential extra damage of his ranged attack. Before making the attack roll, he can choose to spend a use of his *challenge* to deal twice his cavalier level in extra damage on a successful hit instead of just his cavalier level in extra damage. If the attack misses, the use of the *challenge* is wasted." passive="Challenging a foe requires subtle deceits and strategies. The cavalier must make it look like he is a soft target. The subject of the far challenge gains a +4 bonus on attack rolls made against him." special="This challenge remains in effect until the target is dead or unconscious, until the target hits the luring cavalier with a melee attack, or until the combat ends. If this challenge ends because the target hits the luring cavalier with a melee attack, this challenge changes to the effects of the normal cavalier *challenge,* and gains any *order* benefit the luring cavalier has." replace=Challenge}

::ab[Careful Aim]{l=3 icon=ranged-boost passive="When a luring cavalier makes a ranged attack with his highest base attack bonus, he can ignore the penalties for firing up to three range increments away. If he is using a firearm, he can target touch AC up to two range increments away." special="This effect stacks with effects that allow the cavalier to make ranged attacks farther without penalty or that allow him to target touch AC with a firearm beyond the first range increment." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Infuriating Aim (Ex)]{icon=lower l=11 passive="When a luring cavalier confirms a critical hit with a ranged attack made at his highest base attack bonus against the target of a *far challenge,* the target becomes infuriated for 1 round. While infuriated, the target must spend its turn moving closer to the cavalier, making move, ‹rule/run›, or ‹rule/charge› actions only (challenged creature's choice)." special="This is a mind-affecting effect." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Versatile Challenge (Ex)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A luring cavalier can expend a use of his *challenge* to gain the benefit of *far challenge* or the normal cavalier *challenge.* He can even change the type of *challenge* an opponent is under the effects of as a @HLswift_action, as long as the target of the *challenge* is within line of sight." replace="Demanding challenge"}
::ab[Supreme Aim (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost passive="The luring cavalier gains the *careful aim* bonus and the effect of *infuriating aim* on all ranged attacks." replace="Supreme charge"}

musketeer.name: Musketeer
description: ## Musketeer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/38›

Some cavaliers are entrusted by their masters with the care and use of expensive and powerful oddities: firearms. Capable of inflicting shocking amounts of destruction without the aid of magic, these musketeers lead the advance of their troops, firing devastating fusillades long before the enemy can prepare for the armed charge that follows.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down-power note="A musketeer does not gain proficiency in heavy armor. Musketeers are proficient with all simple and martial weapons, and with firearms. A cavalier's levels stack with any fighter levels he possesses for the purpose of meeting the level prerequisite for feats that specifically select a firearm, such as ‹feat/Weapon Specialization›."}

::ab[Gifted Firearm (Ex)]{icon=power-ranged-warning l1="At 1st level, a musketeer is gifted by his order with a firearm (either a ‹eq-weapon/pistol› or a ‹eq-weapon/musket›). This weapon is both a symbol of the cavalier's duty and a focus for much of his talent. He cannot sell this weapon. He also gains the ‹feat/Gunsmithing› feat." l8="When wielding his gifted weapon, as a @HLstandard_action, the mustketeer can focus himself to gain a number of benefits for 1 minute per cavalier level. The musketeer can use this ability twice per day, plus one additional time per day for every four levels beyond 8th. Starting out, the musketeer gains the benefit of the ‹feat/Improved Critical› feat." l11="While focused, he can reduce the misfire chance of his weapon by 1 (to a minimum of 1). This doesn't stack with the similar benefits of ‹magic-enh/reliable› magic weapon special ability." l17="While focused, the musketeer can double the range increment of his weapon. This doesn't stack with the ‹magic-enh/distance› magical ability." l20="During a period of such focus, the musketeer can engage in a flurry of gunfire. The cavalier can expend one of his daily *challenges* to make a full attack with his firearm. The musketeer ignores the normal load times for his weapon, but must still expend enough doses of powder and enough bullets to complete each attack." special="If the musketeer's gifted firearm is destroyed, the cavalier loses this ability for 30 days while a replacement weapon is crafted for him. During this 30-day period, the musketeer takes a -1 penalty on weapon and damage rolls." replace=Mount}

::ab[Swift Powder (Ex)]{icon=power l4="The musketeer gains ‹feat/Rapid Reload› (musket) or Rapid Reload (pistol) as a bonus feat." l14="Each time the musketeer issues a *challenge,* he may reload a wielded firearm as a @HLfree_action." replace="Expert trainer"}

oceanrider.name: Oceanrider
description: ## Oceanrider
‹SOURCE Aquatic Adventures/53›
Oceanriders are underwater cavaliers who ride dolphins, orcas, or seahorses.
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="An oceanrider is proficient with light and medium armor, and with shields (but not tower shields)." alter="Armor proficiency"}

::ab[Aquatic Mount]{icon=power note="A Medium oceanrider can ride either a ‹companion/giant seahorse› or an ‹companion/orca› (unlike most orcas, this one starts at size Large with none of the other level 7 abilities until level 7) instead of the normal options, while a Small oceanrider can ride a ‹companion/dolphin›." alter=Mount replace="Expert trainer"}

qabarat_outrider.name: Qabarat Outrider
description: ## Qabarat Outrider
‹SOURCE People of the Stars/11›

The war colleges of the Castrovelian city-state of Qabarat produce a distinctive breed of cavalier known as the Qabarat outrider. In defense of the lashunta city-states, outriders lead small units of regular troops, the members of which can link minds with one another to form bonds in battle few can match.
::prereq{r=Lashunta}
::ab[Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A Qabarat outrider does not gain proficiency with heavy armor."}
::ab[Combat Expertise]{l=1 icon=power ability="A Qabarat outrider gains ‹feat/Combat Expertise› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites."}

::ab[Maneuver Tactics (Ex)]{icon=power useInc="5~cavalier~~1" imp1="A Qabarat outrider receives an Improved combat maneuver feat (such as ‹feat/Improved Feint›) as a bonus feat. He must meet the prerequisites for this feat. As a @HLstandard_action, a Qabarat outrider can grant this feat to all allies within 30 feet, regardless of whether they can see or hear him or they meet the feat's prerequisites. Allies retain this bonus feat for 3 rounds, plus 1 round for every 2 levels the Qabarat outrider possesses." imp9="A Qabarat outrider gains an Improved or Greater combat maneuver feat (such as ‹feat/Greater Overrun›) for which he meets the prerequisites as a bonus feat. The outrider can grant this feat to his allies using the *maneuver tactics* ability.
