Using the maneuver tactics ability becomes a @HLswift_action." imp17="A Qabarat outrider gains an Improved, Greater, or Strike combat maneuver feat (such as ‹feat/Tripping Strike›) as a bonus feat. He must meet the prerequisites for this feat. An outrider can grant this feat to his allies using the *maneuver tactics* ability." special="A list of &quot&Improved&quot&, &quot&Greater&quot& and &quot&Strike&quot& feats is provided below." replace="Tactician~greater tactician~master tactician"}
### "Improved" Feats
:::div{className=columnar}
- ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush›
- ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick›
- ‹feat/Improved Disarm›
- ‹feat/Improved Drag›
- ‹feat/Improved Feint›
- ‹feat/Improved Grapple›
- ‹feat/Improved Overrun›
- ‹feat/Improved Reposition›
- ‹feat/Improved Steal›
- ‹feat/Improved Sunder›
- ‹feat/Improved Trip›
### "Greater" Feats
:::div{className=columnar}
- ‹feat/Greater Bull Rush›
- ‹feat/Greater Dirty Trick›
- ‹feat/Greater Disarm›
- ‹feat/Greater Drag›
- ‹feat/Greater Feint›
- ‹feat/Greater Grapple›
- ‹feat/Greater Overrun›
- ‹feat/Greater Reposition›
- ‹feat/Greater Steal›
- ‹feat/Greater Sunder›
- ‹feat/Greater Trip›
### "Strike" Feats
:::div{className=columnar}
- ‹feat/Bull Rush Strike›
- ‹feat/Disarming Strike›
- ‹feat/Repositioning Strike›
- ‹feat/Sundering Strike›
- ‹feat/Tripping Strike›

::ab[Mindlink (Su)]{l=5 icon=def-melee passive="A Qabarat outrider's innate telepathy develops into a mindlink, allowing contact between himself and his allies and companions. As long as the Qabarat outrider is conscious, all allies within range of his limited telepathy racial ability (30 feet for most Qabarat outriders) receive a morale bonus (equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› his cavalier level) on saving throws against charm and compulsion effects and a morale bonus (equal to one-fifth his cavalier level) on attack rolls made while ‹rule/flanking›." replace=Banner}

::ab[Mindlink Pulse (Su)]{l=14 icon=boost standard="A Qabarat outrider can send a pulse of psychic energy through the minds of allies within range of his limited telepathy. If he does so during a surprise round, any allies not normally able to act in the surprise round can act. If an ally would normally be denied the ability to act in the surprise round, that ally's initiative is equal to her initiative roll, or to the initiative of the outrider - 1, whichever is lower. Whether or not it is a surprise round, when the outrider uses this ability, allies within range are no longer ‹rule/flat-footed› (assuming they were to begin with)." replace="Greater banner"}

qadiran_horselord.name: Qadiran Horselord
description: ## Qadiran Horselord
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/12;Inner Sea Combat/42›

The traditions of the Qadiran horselords are now spread throughout Qadira and the lands beyond in the Inner Sea region, but they originated among the horse-breeders and mounted-combat specialists of the ‹rule/Al-Zabriti›; the name "horselord" was applied by outlanders. Qadiran horselords pride themselves on their mobility and endurance - qualities also found in the breed of horses they ride. While they don't command the same presence as a host of Lastwall cavalry, Qadiran horselords can get to a battleground in half the time and on a third of the rations. In battle, they are skirmishers, ducking in and out of combat with brutal, precise slashes of their scimitars.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A Qadiran horselord is proficient with all simple and martial weapons, with light and medium armor, and with shields (except tower shields)." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}
::ab[Mount (Ex)]{icon=info note="A Qadiran horselord must select a ‹companion/horse› or ‹companion/pony› as his mount. At the GM's discretion, a different mount can be selected." alter=Mount}
::ab[Desert Wind (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="The speed of a Qadiran horselord's mount increases by 5 feet, plus an additional 5 feet for every 5 cavalier levels he possesses." replace=Tactician}

::ab[Desert Mastery (Ex)]{icon=power l=3 ability="A Qadiran horselord gains ‹ability/favored terrain› (desert) as per the ‹class/ranger› ability of the same name." imp8="The bonus on initiative and skill checks increases by 2, but the Qadiran horselord never selects additional favored terrains." imp13="The bonuses increase by 2 again." imp18="The bonuses increase by 2 once again." passive="His mount gains all the benefits of the favored terrain ability." order=ap replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Sand Storm (Ex)]{icon=power-boost-melee l=6 passive="A Qadiran horselord gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Mobility› feat so long as he is mounted. Additionally, he deals double damage while using a one-handed slashing weapon from the back of a charging mount, as though using a ‹eq-weapon/lance›." replace="The bonus feat gained at 6th level"}

::ab[As One (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power-boost-melee ability="A Qadiran horselord gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Spring Attack› feat so long as he is mounted. He uses his mount's movement for this action, and neither he nor his mount provokes attacks of opportunity from the target. When he makes a single attack with a one-handed slashing weapon in this way, the attack deals 1d6 additional points of damage." imp17="The bonus damage increases to 3d6." special="This ability counts as the Spring Attack feat for the purpose of meeting feat prerequisites, but only while the Qadiran horselord is mounted." replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Sand Scourge (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power-melee fullround="A Qadiran horselord and his mount can make a tactical rush through enemy ranks. His mount can move up to twice its normal speed in a straight line. The Qadiran horselord can make a single attack against each creature he threatens during his mount's movement, up to his normal number of attacks per round. These attacks use his full base attack bonus and take a -2 penalty; this is not a charge, and these attacks deal normal damage. The Qadiran horselord provokes attacks of opportunity during this movement, but his mount does not." replace="Master tactician"}

saurian_champion.name: Saurian Champion
description: ## Saurian Champion
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/45›
These primeval cavaliers idolize dinosaurs as apex predators and value them as powerful, oversized mounts.
::ab[Class Skills]{icon=skill note="A saurian champion gains Acrobatics, Knowledge (nature), Perception, and Survival as class skills." alter="The cavalier's class skills"}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A saurian champion does not gain proficiency with medium or heavy armor." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Savage Combatant (Ex)]{icon=down note="A saurian champion does not rely on lethal charges or firing from afar as more &quot&civilized&quot& cavaliers do, preferring to rampage among his foes instead. Saurian champions do not deal double damage when ‹rule/charging› with a ‹eq-weapon/lance› (or similar weapon) while mounted. While mounted, a saurian champion cannot attack with ranged weapons other than thrown weapons."}

::ab[Titanic Challenge (Ex)]{icon=down-boost ability="A saurian champion considers only the largest creatures a true challenge. His *titanic challenge* adds only half his level on damage rolls against Medium or smaller creatures, but he adds a +1 bonus on attack rolls against a target of his *challenge* that is at least two size categories larger than himself (Huge for Medium saurian champions) and a +2 bonus on attack rolls against a Colossal target of his *challenge.*" alter=Challenge}

::ab[Wild Warrior (Ex)]{icon=down note="A saurian champion hails from primeval lands where the civilized orders hold little sway. He does not select an *order* or gain any *order*-related benefits." replace=Order}
:::ab{icon=power title="Dinosaur Mount (Ex)" action=Ability alter=Mount replace="Tactician]~greater tactician, and @HL[master tactician"}
A saurian champion bonds with a dinosaur companion that willingly serves as his mount. This mount functions as a cavalier's normal mount with the following adjustments:
- The saurian champion's mount must be a dinosaur (see ‹ability/druid companions› for all the available options).
- The saurian champion can ride mounts of his size category and larger, rather than only those of a larger size.
- Increase the mount's Strength score by 8 for the purpose of determining its carrying capacity.
- Whenever an ability or feat allows the saurian champion's mount to make a hoof attack, the mount can make a claw or bite attack instead.

::ab[Nimble Rider (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A saurian champion gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC when mounted. Anything that causes the saurian champion to lose his Dexterity bonus to AC also causes him to lose this dodge bonus." imp8="The bonus becomes +2." imp14="The bonus increases to +3." imp20="This bonus becomes +4."}

::ab[Fierce Devotion (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A saurian champion's mount gains the *devotion* ability (instead of at 6th level), and its effects also apply against emotion and fear effects. The mount is immune to the effects of ‹umr/unnatural aura›." replace=Banner}

::ab[Quick Rider (Ex)]{l=8 icon=power ability="A saurian champion can attempt ‹skill/Ride› checks to fast mount or fast dismount his mount even if it's more than one size category larger than himself."}

::ab[Titanic Mount (Ex)]{l=10 icon=boost passive="A saurian champion's mount increases in size by one category (even if the base creature normally does not reach that size). The creature receives the appropriate size penalty on attack rolls and to AC (-1 if growing to Large or -2 if growing to Huge) and a -2 penalty to Dexterity (to a minimum Dexterity score of 1), its base damage increases by ‹misc/one size category›, and its reach increases to 10 feet (if it wasn't already). It also gains a +2 size bonus to its Strength and Constitution score." imp12="The bonus to Strength becomes +4 and his mount's natural armor bonus increases by +1." imp14="The Strength bonus becomes +6, the Constitution bonus becomes +4, and the natural armor now increases by +2." imp15="The mount's reach increases to 15 feet." imp16="The Strength bonus becomes +8, and the natural armor now increases by +3." imp18="The Strength bonus becomes +10, the Constitution bonus becomes +6, and the natural armor now increases by +4."}

::ab[Primeval Devotion (Ex)]{l=14 icon=def passive="The bonus on saving throws provided by the mount's *devotion* ability increases by 2. In addition, the mount adds half this bonus to other saving throws." replace="Greater banner"}

sister_in_arms.name: Sister-in-Arms
description: ## Sister-in-Arms
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/83›

Some of the most charismatic ‹rule/Gray Maidens› combine their devotion to their sisters with the intense tactical training they received, learning to perfectly direct their companions and inspiring them to survive and emerge victorious. Known as sisters-in-arms, these scarred soldiers are highly respected by their fellow Maidens for their dedication.

In the aftermath of Ileosa's fall, groups of Maidens forged a tight-knit family from their fellow survivors, knowing that nobody else truly understood the horrors they had endured - or had been forced to commit. Even after being accepted back into Korvosan society or fleeing the reach of the city's justice, the Gray Maidens' sisterhood has endured.

Sisters-in-arms are almost never neutral on the subject of Queen Ileosa; some still hold true to the cruel queen's ideals and lead bands of like-minded veterans, while others have forsaken Ileosa in favor of total devotion to their fellow survivors. Of the latter, the majority support the Scarlet Rose, whether as social and emotional support for their recovering sisters, as field commanders for military endeavors, or as private adventurers with ties to the order. Loyalist sisters-in-arms, by contrast, are almost always plotting to reclaim Korvosa.

::ab[Half-hearted Challenge (Ex)]{icon=down note="Sisters-in-arms vigilantly protect their allies, weakening their focus on their challenged foe. A sister-in-arms adds only half her cavalier level (minimum 1) to damage rolls against her *challenge* target." alter=Challenge}

:::ab{title="Maiden's Order (Ex)" icon=info-power action=Ability order=Cc choice="A sister-in-arms chooses which order's edicts she must follow; generally, those of the fanged crown choose the order of the lion's edicts, while those of the scarlet rose choose the order of the dragon's edicts." alter=Order replace=Mount}

All sisters-in-arms share a talent for aiding and inspiring their fellow Gray Maidens, strengthened by training and the bond of sisterhood. Generally, those loyal to Queen Ileosa refer to their pledge as the *order of the fanged crown,* while those who have forsaken the cruel queen call it the *order of the scarlet rose.* In either case, the benefits are the same.

A sister-in-arms does not choose an *order.* Instead, she is considered to belong to both the ‹order/order of the dragon› and the ‹order/order of the lion›, and she gains all of the benefits of both orders (including additional class skills and conditional skill bonuses, additional *challenge* benefits, and order abilities) at the appropriate levels.

::ab[Devoted Defender (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power flavor="Sisters-in-arms cling to their fellow Gray Maidens as closely as family and protect them with intense devotion." ability="A sister-in-arms gains ‹feat/Bodyguard› as a bonus feat (she does not need to meet the feat's prerequisite)." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Maiden's Loyalty (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def flavor="The loyalty of a sister-in-arms is absolute." passive="A sister-in-arms gains a bonus on Will saves against any effect that would compel her to attack or betray her allies. This bonus is equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› her cavalier level." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Dedicated Commander (Ex)]{flavor="A sister-in-arms can inspire and direct allies with great efficiency." l=11 icon=boost ability="The sister-in-arms can use *lion's call* (lion) or *strategy order* (dragon) as a @HLmove_action, rather than as a standard action." imp20="She can use *act as one* (dragon), *lion's call* (lion), or *strategy order* (dragon) as a @HLswift or @HLmove_action." replace="Mighty charge~supreme charge"}

spellscar_drifter.name: Spellscar Drifter
description: ## Spellscar Drifter
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/45›

The Spellscar Desert is a dangerous wasteland into which few venture without a guide. Spellscar drifters have made themselves at home in these badlands. When not scouring the desert for forgotten treasures or magic hidden by the region's shifting magic-dead zones, a Spellscar drifter might hire himself out as a guide, bodyguard, or assassin. There are even a few Spellscar drifters who are temporarily deputized by the Grand Duchy of Alkenstar to perform law enforcement tasks beyond the reach of the city's normal constabulary. Spellscar drifters are self-reliant and always keep their firearms loaded - in the Spellscar Desert, a rider with a slow draw doesn't live long.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="Spellscar drifters are proficient with all simple and martial weapons, the ‹eq-weapon/whip›, and with all ‹main/equipment_>firearms›. They are proficient with all light armor." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Have Gun]{icon=power l=1 ability="The Spellscar drifter gains ‹feat/Amateur Gunslinger› and ‹feat/Gunsmithing› as bonus feats. The Spellscar drifter uses his Charisma modifier (minimum 1) in place of his Wisdom modifier when determining his maximum grit points and for any other abilities which use grit. Additionally, he also gains a battered gun identical to the one gained by the ‹class/gunslinger›." replace=Tactician}

::ab[Daring Deeds (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power choice="A Spellscar drifter gains ‹feat/Rapid Reload› as a bonus feat. The Spellscar drifter must choose a firearm to associate with this feat. Additionally, the Spellscar drifter can select one additional ‹ability/1st-level »gunslinger deed<s› which he can use with his Amateur Gunslinger feat." replace="Cavalier's charge"}

::ab[Worn Banner (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A Spellscar drifter can choose to use his hat as his banner. If the drifter does not wear a hat, another iconic accessory - such as an eyemask, bandana, or distinguishing coat - can instead serve as his banner. In all other ways, this ability functions as the *banner* cavalier ability." alter=Banner}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{l=6 icon=power ability="A Spellscar drifter gains a bonus feat in addition to those gained from normal advancement. This bonus feat must be either a ‹main/combat<_feat› or ‹main/feats_>grit« feat›, and the Spellscar drifter must meet the prerequisites as normal." imp12="The cavalier gains another bonus feat, following the restrictions above." imp18="The Spellscar drifter gains a third such feat." replace="The cavalier's normal bonus feats"}

::ab[Notorious Deeds (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power choice="A Spellscar drifter can select two additional gunslinger deeds which he can use with his Amateur Gunslinger feat. The Spellscar drifter can choose one deed from the list of deeds available to 3rd-level gunslingers and one from the list available to 7th-level gunslingers." replace="Greater tactician"}

::ab[Old Reliable (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost order=pa passive="Whenever a Spellscar drifter declares a *challenge,* the threat range of the Spellscar drifter's firearm is doubled against the target of his *challenge.* This increase only affects one weapon and does not stack with other effects that increase the threat range of the weapon." ability="If the Spellscar drifter rolls a misfire during his *challenge,* the Spellscar drifter can spend 1 grit point to reroll the attack roll. The Spellscar drifter must accept the results of the second roll, even if it is worse." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Spell Severed (Ex)]{l=12 icon=def order=pa passive="A Spellscar drifter becomes permanently marked by long exposure to the Spellscar Desert. The Spellscar drifter gains spell resistance equal to 10 + his character level." ability="The Spellscar drifter can drop this spell resistance for 1 round as a @HLstandard_action, or he can spend 1 grit point to drop it as a @HLswift_action." replace="Demanding challenge"}

::ab[Infamous Deeds (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power choice="A Spellscar drifter can select two additional gunslinger deeds which he can use with his Amateur Gunslinger feat. The Spellscar drifter can choose one deed from the list of deeds available to 7th-level gunslingers and one from the list available to 11th-level gunslingers." replace="Master tactician"}

::ab[Tough as Nails (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power-boost order=cp choice="A Spellscar drifter is able to select three additional deeds for use with his Amateur Gunslinger feat - two from the list of deeds available to 15th-level gunslingers, and one from the list available to 19th-level gunslingers." passive="If the Spellscar drifter confirms a critical hit on an attack made with a firearm, the target is ‹misc/stunned› for 1d4 rounds. A successful Will saving throw (DC = 10 + the Spellscar drifter's base attack bonus) reduces this effect to ‹misc/staggered› instead of stunned." replace="Supreme charge"}

standard_bearer.name: Standard Bearer
description: ## Standard Bearer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/39›

Not all cavaliers are content to ride at the head of a charge, leading from the front and facing down their enemies directly. Some prefer to stand away from the fray, their banners a beacon shining brightly over the battlefield, rallying their troops to victory. The standard bearer employs the banner of his order, his lord, or his own house to raise the spirits of his allies and warns enemies of impending doom.

::ab[Banner (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A standard bearer gains the *banner* ability. This ability is identical to the standard cavalier's *banner* ability, except that the morale bonus on saving throws against fear effects is equal to 2 + ‹misc/one-fifth› your cavalier level, and the morale bonus on attack rolls made as part of a charge is equal to 1 + one-fifth your cavalier level." replace=Mount}
::ab[Mount (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power ability="A standard bearer gains the service of a loyal and trusted mount. This mount is identical in all ways to the standard cavalier's mount." replace=Banner}

::ab[Banner of Solace (Ex)]{l=11 icon=boost-def flavor="A standard bearer's banner becomes an even more potent symbol of protection and inspiration to those around him." fullround="Once per day, while his banner is displayed, the standard bearer can wave it through the air, granting all allies within 60 feet ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to 1/2 his cavalier level, and a +2 morale bonus on their next damage rolls. The temporary hit points last for 10 minutes or until depleted, whichever occurs first." imp15="The morale bonus becomes +3." imp19="The morale bonus becomes +4." replace="Mighty charge"}

::ab[Awesome Pennon (Ex)]{l=20 icon=boost-def flavor="A standard bearer's banner has become a powerful rallying point to his allies, and a bane to his foes." passive="Whenever his banner is visible, allies of the standard bearer within 60 feet gain a +1 morale bonus on attack rolls, immunity to fear effects, and a +3 morale bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects." replace="Supreme charge"}

strategist.name: Strategist
description: ## Strategist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/39›

Some cavaliers make a lifelong mission out of their exceptional ability to direct troops on the battlefield, combining tactical insight with a preternatural skill at improving the teamwork of their allies in order to win the day. Such strategists are masters at learning the strengths and weaknesses of those they fight alongside, and at employing those observations at the most opportune moments. The best of them are also skilled at determining the strengths and weaknesses of their enemies, as well as at countering some of those strengths with their own formidable abilities.

::ab[Tactician (Ex)]{icon=boost l=1 ability="As the cavalier class feature, except that a strategist can use this ability once per day, plus one additional time for every four levels after 1st (to a maximum of five times per day at 17th level)." alter=Tactician}

::ab[Drill Instructor (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A strategist learns to train his fellow adventurers in the nuances of squad combat. By spending 10 minutes and expending 1 use of his *challenge* ability, the strategist can grant the use of a ‹main/teamwork feat› that he knows to up to four of his allies, similar to the *tactician* ability. As with *tactician,* allies need not meet the prerequisites of the granted feat, but they retain the use of this feat for 10 minutes plus 1 minute for every two levels the cavalier possesses, as long as the cavalier is visible and can be heard by his allies." special="If the cavalier falls unconscious or cannot be both seen and heard, his allies lose the benefit of the granted feat until the condition is remedied." replace="Expert trainer"}

::ab[Tactical Advantage (Ex)]{l=14 icon=power free="Whenever a strategist uses his *tactician* ability to grant the use of a teamwork feat to his allies, he can move up to his speed either before or after granting the feat. This movement provokes attacks of opportunity normally." replace="Greater banner"}

::ab[Strategic Supremacy (Ex)]{l=18 icon=power-lower ability="A strategist gains the ability not only to improve his allies' abilities, but also to disrupt the teamwork of his enemies. Whenever the strategist uses the *tactician* ability, he can choose to cancel out the effects of one teamwork feat employed by any of his opponents within 30 feet instead of granting the use of a teamwork feat to his allies." replace="The cavalier's 18th-level bonus feat"}

verdivant.name: Verdivant
description: ## Verdivant
‹SOURCE Wilderness Origins/15›

Some ‹race/vine leshy› spirits are vibrant enough to project this animus beyond their own bodies. Called verdivants, these leshys explore the world atop unique companions formed from the leshy's vines and animated by their nearly boundless energy. Both mount and rider brim with new growth, sporting bright flowers, whipping tendrils, clouds of pollen, and other manifestations charged with nature's power. This archetype is typically available only to vine leshys, but with the GM's permission, other races can take it as well.
:::ab{title="Plant Mount (Ex)" icon=power action=Ability alter=Mount replace="Expert trainer" imp4="Regrowing a mount takes only 24 hours."}

A verdivant buds off and cultivates his vines into an animal form, imbuing them with limited intelligence. The verdivant selects a mount appropriate for his size as normal, but this mount is a plant with the ‹subtype/leshy› subtype and has the same immunities as a vine leshy.

A verdivant can absorb and regrow his plant mount. This can either replace a slain mount or change a mount's form; the new form must still be a suitable mount the verdivant is capable of riding. Either function takes 1 week, after which the newly regrown mount immediately benefits from its special abilities.

::ab[Effloresce]{l=2 head useInc="4~cavalier~2~3" icon=power standard="A verdivant can create *efflorescences,* explosive growths fed by his animating spirit. A verdivant can apply *effloresces* to himself, or his plant mount if it is adjacent. *Efflorescences* last for 1 round per cavalier level, and a verdivant can have at most two *efflorescences* in effect at a time. A verdivant must meet the level requirement to use one of these abilities." free="A verdivant can end an *efflorescence* during his turn." replace="Tactician~banner~greater tactician~greater banner~master tactician~bonus feats" imp6="A verdivant can effloresce as a @HLmove_action." imp12="A verdivant can effloresce as a @HLswift_action, and he can have two *efflorescences* in effect simultaneously." imp17="A verdivant can effloresce twice as part of the same action." order=usf}
::ab[Shieldvines (Ex)]{next icon=def-aura l2="Allies within 10 feet do not provoke attacks of opportunity when leaving threatened squares." l9="This increases to 20 feet."}

::ab[Floatflower (Ex)]{next icon=boost-aura l2="Allies within 10 feet ignore the effects of nonmagical ‹rule/difficult terrain›." l9="They also gain the effects of ‹spell/air walk› while within 10 feet of the verdivant."}

::ab[Innervating Pollen (Ex)]{next icon=boost-def-aura l5="The verdivant and his allies within 30 feet gain a competence bonus either on attack rolls or to saving throws (the verdivant's choice; all allies gain the same benefit) equal to ‹misc/one-third› the verdivant's cavalier level."}

::ab[Bolsterbloom (Su)]{next icon=aid-aura l5="The verdivant and his allies within 30 feet gain fast healing 1." imp9="This becomes fast healing 2." l14="This becomes fast healing 3." l17="This becomes fast healing 4."}

::ab[Charged Blossoms (Su)]{next icon=def-aura l9="Choose an energy type: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. The verdivant and his allies within 10 feet gain energy resistance 10 to the chosen energy type." l14="This affects allies within 20 feet, and the energy resistance increases to 20."}
::ab[Slipstrands (Su)]{next icon=boost-aura l9="The verdivant and his allies within 10 feet are affected by ‹spell/freedom of movement›." l17="This increases to 20 feet."}

::ab[Ambrosia (Su)]{next icon=def-aura l14="The *efflorescence* temporarily suppresses enchantments, transmutations, and curses on the verdivant and his allies within 30 feet, as ‹spell/break enchantment› with a caster level equal to the verdivant's cavalier level. Affected creatures ignore the effects of such conditions for the duration of the *efflorescence,* after which the conditions' full effects resume. The duration of suppressed conditions continues to elapse while the condition is suppressed."}

::ab[Luckleaf (Su)]{next icon=roll-aura l14="When attempting a d20 roll, allies of the verdivant within 30 feet of him can roll twice and use the better result; each ally can benefit from this ability only once per *efflorescence.*" special="Luckleaf can be activated once per minute."}

::ab[Sinuous Steps (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-def ability="A verdivant and his plant mount gain the ‹ability/woodland stride› druid class feature. In addition, they do not take any penalty to AC after making a charge attack." alter="Cavalier's charge"}

vermin_tamer.name: Vermin Tamer
description: ## Vermin Tamer
‹SOURCE Heroes of the Darklands/17›

Due to the strange terrain, the lack of sunlight, and numerous other hazards, keeping a traditional mount in the Darklands can prove difficult. Cavaliers native to or traveling the region may choose to tame local creatures instead. Those who can overcome their revulsion and distrust of Sekamina's strange fauna often find that such partnerships with native creatures serve both rider and mount well. While these mounts may be harder to handle than typical mounts, their versatility and adaptations prove indispensable.
:::ab{l=1 icon=power title="Darklands Mount (Ex)" action=Ability alter=Mount}

A vermin tamer chooses a creature native to the Darklands as his mount. This functions as a cavalier's mount. He can choose a ‹companion/giant beetle›, ‹companion/giant centipede›, ‹companion/giant gecko›, ‹companion/giant slug›, or ‹companion/giant spider› as his mount. These mounts (except the giant gecko) and the rules for vermin companions are detailed [here, under Vermin Companions](ability/druid_companions).

The selected mount is combat trained. The vermin trainer does not take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while riding his mount. If the vermin tamer's starting mount is large enough to act as a mount, it gains ‹feat/Light Armor Proficiency› as a bonus feat. If the selected animal companion is too small to act as a proper mount, then the vermin tamer gains ‹feat/Undersized Mount› as a bonus feat.

::ab[Stuck in the Saddle (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="A practiced vermin tamer can remain steady in the saddle even in unconventional situations." l4="He gains a +4 circumstance bonus on any checks to stay in the saddle when his mount is climbing walls or flying." l8="This bonus increases to +8, and he never falls off his mount when he is rendered unconscious." replace="Expert training"}

::ab[Disorienting Challenge (Ex)]{icon=lower l=12 passive="Whenever a vermin tamer declares a *challenge,* he can confound his target with the erratic movements of his mount. As long as the vermin tamer begins his turn mounted, with his mount climbing or flying at a higher location than his target, the target of his *challenge* takes a -2 penalty to its AC." replace="Demanding challenge"}

wave_rider.name: Wave Rider
description: ## Wave Rider
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/47›

Throughout the Inner Sea region, wave riders patrol harbors, keeping them safe from dangerous sea creatures and watching for smugglers. The most famous of these is the hippocampus-riding Sea Cavalry of Absalom.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="Wave riders are proficient with all simple and martial weapons and with light and medium armor, but not with shields." replace="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Seafaring Companion]{l=1 icon=power ability="A wave rider gains ‹feat/Monstrous Mount› as a bonus feat. The wave rider can use this feat only to select a ‹companion/hippocampus« mount›. Otherwise, this ability functions identically to the cavalier's *mount* ability." alter=Mount}
